title: Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship 2026 for African Women Scientists (Africa–Australia Partnership)
summary: African women scientists working in agriculture and climate research can now apply for the Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship 2026 , a prestigious leadership and career development program focused on climate-resilient agriculture and gender-responsive agrifood systems . The fellowship is implemented by African Women in Agricultural Research and Development (AWARD) under the Africa Australia Partnership for Climate Responsive Agriculture and aims to strengthen the leadership capacity of women researchers driving climate adaptation and sustainable agriculture across Africa. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Key Details Fellowship: Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship Organizer: African Women in Agricultural Research and Development (AWARD) Program Duration: 24 months Eligible Countries: Egypt, Ghana, Morocco, Nigeria, Senegal, Sierra Leone Target Participants: African women scientists in agriculture and climate research Degree Requirement: Minimum Master s degree in a relevant discipline Experience Required: At least 7 years of professional experience Language Requirement: English or French proficiency Application Fee: None (Free to apply) Application Deadline: April 26, 2026 (23:59 East Africa Time GMT+3) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship The Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship is designed to strengthen the capability, confidence, and influence of African women scientists working in agriculture and climate research. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Through leadership development, mentoring partnerships, and technical learning opportunities, the fellowship prepares women researchers to become leaders in climate-resilient agriculture and agrifood systems innovation . The program draws inspiration from the successful AWARD Fellowship Model , connecting participants with a broader ecosystem of agricultural researchers, mentors, and leaders. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will participate in a comprehensive professional development program that includes: Leadership and negotiation training Mentoring partnerships with both mentors and mentees Training on gender integration in climate adaptation Science communication and publishing courses Networking with agricultural and climate research experts Collaboration with AWARD alumni and partner networks The fellowship will engage up to 300 beneficiaries , including: 100 Fellows 100 Mentors 100 Mentees Eligibility Criteria To apply, applicants must: Be African women scientists working in agriculture, climate science, or agrifood systems Have a minimum of seven years of professional experience in agriculture, climate research, gender integration, or related fields ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Hold at least a master s degree in a relevant discipline such as: Agriculture Agricultural Economics Agricultural Extension Natural Resource Management Food Science and Nutrition Climate Science Be nationals of the target countries and residing in Africa Demonstrate strong interest in advancing gender-responsive and climate-resilient research Be proficient in English or French ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Applicants must also show the potential to influence research, policy, and innovation in agrifood systems . Program Approach The program adopts a two-track implementation approach : Fellowship Track This track focuses on career development for women scientists , providing mentorship, leadership training, and professional growth opportunities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Institutional Strengthening Track This component supports national agricultural research systems (NARS) in integrating gender-responsive approaches into institutional policies and research programs for equitable climate action . Target Participants Participants will be drawn from institutions across the agricultural research ecosystem, including: National Agricultural Research Institutions (NARIs) Ministries of Agriculture Universities and academic institutions International agricultural research organizations Climate and agrifood research centers How to Apply Applications must be submitted online through the official fellowship application portal . Applicants should apply through the appropriate portal depending on their language preference: English-speaking applicants: Apply through the English application portal here French-speaking applicants: Apply through the French application portal here ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Applicants living with disabilities who require reasonable adjustments during the application process can contact: please email T.Wangaya@cifor-icraf.org . There is no application or registration fee . Application Deadline: April 26, 2026 (23:59 East Africa Time GMT+3) Late applications will not be accepted . For more information, please download and read: Fellowship Brochure Frequently Asked Questions Interested candidates are invited to apply by April 26, 2026 , at 23:59 East Africa Time (GMT+3). Applications will NOT be accepted after this date. Apply Now About the Africa Australia Partnership for Climate Responsive Agriculture The Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship is funded through the Africa Australia Partnership for Climate Responsive Agriculture . The partnership aims to strengthen agricultural research capacity and increase the number of women leading climate-resilient agriculture initiatives across Africa . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); The program is delivered by the Australian Centre for International Agricultural Research (ACIAR) in collaboration with the Australian Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT) . Why This Fellowship Matters Women scientists are critical to transforming Africa s agricultural systems, climate resilience strategies, and food security efforts . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); By investing in leadership development and research capacity, the fellowship helps ensure that women researchers play a central role in shaping sustainable, inclusive, and climate-resilient agrifood systems across Africa . Acknowledgement The Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship is an initiative of African Women in Agricultural Research and Development (AWARD) funded under the Africa Australia Partnership for Climate Responsive Agriculture. It seeks to contribute to the Partnership s end-of-program outcome two (EOPO2): Australia leverages its funding to national and multilateral agricultural research institutes to increase the number of women leading and participating in climate resilient agriculture . The Africa Australia Partnership is delivered by the Australian Centre for International Agricultural Research (ACIAR), in collaboration with the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade. Source https://awardfellowships.org/news/call-to-apply-for-the-women-in-agriculture-leadership-program-fellowship-for-the-africa-australia-partnership-for-climate-responsive-agriculture/ The post Women in Agriculture Leadership Program Fellowship 2026 for African Women Scientists (Africa–Australia Partnership) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: $9 Million Cigna Foundation Grant for Youth Mental Health Programs (Apply by March 12, 2026)
summary: Nonprofit organizations working to support children and teenagers can now apply for the Improving Youth Mental Health Grant Program , funded by the Cigna Group Foundation . The foundation has committed $9 million over three years to support programs that improve youth mental health, emotional well-being, and access to mental health care for young people aged 5 18 . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); This funding opportunity focuses on addressing the growing mental health challenges affecting children and adolescents, particularly post-pandemic stress, anxiety, and emotional distress . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Organizations working with parents, caregivers, educators, therapists, and youth-serving professionals are also encouraged to apply. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Application Deadline: March 12, 2026 (5:00 PM ET) About the Improving Youth Mental Health Grant Program The Improving Youth Mental Health Grant Program supports nonprofit organizations delivering evidence-informed programs that help children and teens build resilience, develop social-emotional skills, and access mental health services. The initiative responds to the growing need for early intervention and mental health support among young people. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Even before the COVID-19 pandemic, one in five children aged 3 17 experienced a mental, emotional, developmental, or behavioral disorder . Since the pandemic, the need for effective mental health support systems for youth has increased significantly. The Cigna Foundation aims to partner with organizations that can deliver scalable solutions, strengthen community support systems, and expand access to care for underserved youth populations . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Grant Focus Areas Nonprofits applying for funding must align their programs with at least one of the following priority goals : 1. Strengthening Social-Emotional Skills Programs that help youth develop skills related to: Emotional regulation Stress management Resilience Mental well-being 2. Supporting Parents, Caregivers, and Youth Professionals Initiatives that equip adults with the tools and knowledge to support youth mental health, including: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Educators Youth workers Therapists Parents and guardians 3. Expanding Access to Mental Health Care Programs that improve pathways to mental health intervention, including: Early identification of mental health challenges Access to counseling or therapy School-based mental health services Community mental health programs Examples of Funded Youth Mental Health Programs The Cigna Foundation has previously supported a wide range of impactful initiatives through this grant program. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); 2024 Partner Projects Examples of impact through funded grant programs from our 2024 nonprofit partners include: A holistic, trauma-informed after-school program designed to empower teen boys and girls severely impacted by challenging life circumstances. Building social-emotional learning skills in high-school students and educating parents, caregivers, and educators about specific mental health challenges of the students. Subsidized therapy for youth, as well as mental health training for local youth-serving organizations and schools. Explore outcomes from this cohort in our Improving Youth Mental Health 2025 Impact Report . Examples of impact through funded grant programs from our 2025 nonprofit partners include: A virtual program led by medical students that helps youth in grades 6-12 build skills to manage their stress, develop an understanding of how to take care of their own mental health, and respond to peers experiencing suicidal thoughts. A Students Supporting Students program that recruits and trains peers, supported by school staff, to identify signs and symptoms of mental health issues and empowers them to lead a mental health campaign in their school. Cost-free, equitable access to school-based care, within the school setting and during school hours, using personalized treatment plans. Who Should Apply This grant opportunity is designed for nonprofit organizations that serve children and adolescents experiencing mental health challenges . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Ideal applicants include organizations that: Provide youth mental health services Deliver school-based mental health programs Support social-emotional learning Train parents, caregivers, and educators Offer early intervention or therapy services for youth Work with underserved or high-need communities Programs should be evidence-informed and focused on measurable outcomes for youth well-being. Why This Grant Matters Investing in youth mental health today helps build healthier, more resilient communities tomorrow . The Cigna Foundation believes that supporting the mental well-being of young people strengthens families, communities, and society as a whole. Through partnerships with nonprofits, the foundation aims to expand access to care, strengthen support networks, and ensure that children and teens have the resources they need to thrive . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); How to Apply Organizations interested in applying for funding can submit their applications before the deadline. The Improving Youth Mental Health Grant application is open now until March 12, 2026 at 5 pm ET. Download the Request for Application (RFA) [PDF] for details about the grant program. Register and apply now Learn More Read our frequently asked questions . Related Links The Cigna Group Foundation Related Resources Improving Youth Mental Health 2025 Impact Report 2025 Youth Mental Health Press Release 2024 Youth Mental Health Press Release ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); 1 Protecting Youth Mental Health, Department of Health and Human Services, 2021, https://www.hhs.gov/sites/default/files/surgeon-general-youth-mental-health-advisory.pdf Source https://www.thecignagroup.com/our-impact/esg/healthy-society/community/foundation/improving-youth-mental-health The post $9 Million Cigna Foundation Grant for Youth Mental Health Programs (Apply by March 12, 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Africa CDC Kofi Annan Scholars Program 2026: Strategy Development Expert (AVoHC) – Apply by 2 April
summary: Public health professionals across Africa and the African diaspora can now apply for the Africa CDC Kofi Annan Scholars Program Strategy Development Expert role under the African Volunteers Health Corps (AVoHC) . This prestigious opportunity deploys experienced experts to support national public health systems, strategic planning, and health policy development across Africa . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The programme contributes to Africa s Health Security and Sovereignty agenda , strengthening the continent s ability to prevent, detect, and respond to public health threats. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Key Details Organization: Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) Programme: Kofi Annan Scholars Program African Volunteers Health Corps (AVoHC) Role: Strategy Development Expert Number of Positions: 20 Deployment Type: On-call expert roster Contract Type: Volunteer roster with paid deployments Location: Deployments across African Union Member States Application Deadline: 2 April 2026 (11:59 PM EAT) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Successful applicants will join a roster of Strategy Development Experts who may be deployed to support Ministries of Health, National Public Health Institutes (NPHIs), and public health institutions across Africa. About the Kofi Annan Global Health Leadership Program The Kofi Annan Global Health Leadership Program (KA-GHLP) was launched by the African Union Commission and Africa CDC in partnership with the Kofi Annan Foundation . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The initiative aims to build a new generation of African public health leaders capable of strengthening health systems and improving pandemic preparedness across the continent. The program includes three main components: Public Health Leadership Fellowship Public Health Scholars Program Public Health Policy Forum The Kofi Annan Scholars Program specifically deploys experienced professionals into time-bound technical assignments to strengthen national health systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Role of the Strategy Development Expert Strategy Development Experts will support governments and public health institutions in designing and implementing evidence-based health strategies and institutional frameworks. These assignments may involve developing: National public health workforce strategies Institutional development plans Health system strengthening frameworks Emergency preparedness and response strategies ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Experts will also ensure alignment with Africa CDC s New Public Health Order and national priorities. Key Responsibilities Selected experts will: Conduct situational analyses and capability assessments Identify policy and operational gaps in public health systems Draft national strategies and institutional plans Facilitate strategic planning consultations and stakeholder workshops Develop implementation roadmaps and monitoring frameworks Produce technical reports, policy briefs, and planning guidance Support resource mobilization and partner coordination Design monitoring, evaluation, and learning (MEL) systems ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Eligibility Requirements Academic Qualifications Applicants must hold a: Master s or PhD in Public Health Health Policy Health Systems Management Strategic Planning or related field Professional Experience Applicants should have: At least 7 years of experience in strategic planning or public health policy Experience working with Ministries of Health, NPHIs, or development partners Demonstrated ability to develop national strategies or institutional frameworks Knowledge of African health systems and policy environments Experience with Africa CDC initiatives or African Union frameworks such as Agenda 2063 is an advantage. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Required Skills Technical Skills Policy analysis and strategic planning Institutional development and system strengthening Stakeholder engagement and facilitation Strategic writing and policy communication Personal Competencies Adaptability in complex environments Strong cultural awareness and emotional intelligence Ability to work independently and in teams Commitment to service and impact Language Requirements Applicants must be proficient in at least one African Union working language: English French Arabic Portuguese Spanish Swahili Knowledge of multiple AU languages is an advantage. Benefits of Joining the AVoHC Roster Although membership in the African Volunteers Health Corps (AVoHC) does not provide payment during the roster period, experts receive benefits during deployments. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); During Deployment Round-trip travel tickets Health insurance Stipend and allowances Accommodation allowance (when applicable) Communications and incidentals support Certificate of recognition Additional Opportunities Professional development training Mentorship opportunities Networking with experts from 55 African Union member states Participation in high-impact public health initiatives across Africa ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Application Requirements Applicants must submit: Completed African Union CV Template Updated CV Copy of African passport Academic certificates and qualifications Only fully completed applications will be considered. Application Deadline 2 April 2026, 11:59 PM EAT Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Gender Equality and Inclusion Africa CDC is an equal opportunity employer , and qualified women are strongly encouraged to apply . Candidates from underrepresented African Union member states are also encouraged to apply. How to Apply Interested applicants can apply through the official African Union recruitment portal: APPLY HERE FOR MORE DETAILS PLEASE VISIT The post Africa CDC Kofi Annan Scholars Program 2026: Strategy Development Expert (AVoHC) – Apply by 2 April appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Odysseus Summer School 2026 Doctoral Seminar & Grants for PhD Students in EU Migration and Asylum Law
summary: PhD researchers working on European migration and asylum law have a unique opportunity to present their research, receive expert feedback, and potentially receive financial support through the 2026 Odysseus Summer School Doctoral Seminar. Organized by the Odysseus Academic Network for Legal Studies on Immigration and Asylum in Europe, the doctoral seminar forms part of the prestigious Odysseus Summer School program. It offers selected doctoral candidates the opportunity to engage with leading experts in migration and asylum law while strengthening their research through constructive academic dialogue. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The program is hosted by the Odysseus Network at Universit libre de Bruxelles (ULB) and aims to foster collaboration and academic excellence in European migration and asylum policy research. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the Doctoral Seminar The doctoral seminar is designed to support PhD researchers by providing a platform to present their thesis projects to a panel of experts drawn from members of the Odysseus Network. Participants receive detailed feedback on their research topic, structure, and methodology. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The seminar is primarily aimed at PhD students in law. However, doctoral researchers from related fields such as political science or international relations are also welcome to apply, provided their research addresses legal aspects of migration or asylum. Participants benefit from: Direct feedback from renowned academics and specialists in migration and asylum law Advice to strengthen the research design and methodology of their doctoral thesis Networking opportunities with fellow researchers and experts across Europe Exposure to current debates in EU migration and asylum law and policy ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Financial Support: Four PhD Grants Available The Odysseus Network offers four competitive grants to support PhD students attending the summer school and doctoral seminar. The grant covers: Tuition fees for the summer school (excluding the 350 deposit) Travel expenses Accommodation costs ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Please note that daily subsistence and personal expenses are not included. Grant recipients will be selected based on both their academic record and financial need. Successful applicants will also assist the coordination team with on-site organization during the summer school. This role does not interfere with their participation in the academic sessions. Eligibility Requirement Participation in the doctoral seminar is limited to individuals who have already registered for the Odysseus Summer School and paid the required deposit fee. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Applicants seeking financial support must clearly explain their need for funding as part of the application process. Application Requirements Applicants must submit a complete application package that includes: Cover page including the applicant s name, PhD title, year of research progress, and supervisor s email address ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Curriculum Vitae with detailed academic background Academic records for each year of study (if applicable) A one-page summary of the PhD research topic explaining its originality and contribution to existing knowledge A one-page overview outlining the structure of the doctoral thesis Proof of payment of the 350 deposit for the Summer School Letter of motivation (required for applicants requesting financial support) All documents must be merged into a single PDF file before submission. Application Process To apply for the doctoral seminar and grants, applicants must: Complete the registration form for the Odysseus Summer School. Carefully fill out the PhD catalogue on the Odysseus website. Submit the full application package by email to: odysseus@ulb.be Incomplete applications will not be considered. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more grant opportunities, click here . Application Deadline The deadline to submit applications is 15 May 2026 . Prospective applicants are encouraged to prepare their documents early to ensure a complete and competitive submission. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Contact Information For additional details regarding the doctoral seminar or application process, applicants may contact: Odysseus Network Universit libre de Bruxelles (ULB) Email: odysseus@ulb.be The post Odysseus Summer School 2026 Doctoral Seminar Grants for PhD Students in EU Migration and Asylum Law appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: GOAL NextGen Youth Programme 2026: Global Leadership Programme for Young Changemakers (Stipend Available)
summary: GOAL s NextGen Youth Programme brings together young people from all over the world to better understand global justice issues and deepen their understanding of Global Citizenship. This programme will guide young, motivated changemakers to learn about the growing crises facing our world with a focus on climate and health systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Participants will then work together to imagine solutions for the future by creating awareness-raising materials that will feed into GOAL s public engagement activities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); When will it take place? The 2026 Programme will consist of 10 sessions that take place online, with 2 sessions in-person for Irish based participants. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Programme Dates: Sat May 23 Irish based participants in-person 11-4pm. T ue May 26 International participants online 3-5pm Thu June 4 All participants online 3-5pm Thu June 11 All participants online 3-5pm Saturday- Sunday June 20/21 Irish based participants in person retreat. Time and location TBC. Thu June 25 All participants online 3-5pm Thu July 2 All participants online 3-5pm Thu July 9 All participants online 3-5pm Thu July 16 All participants online 3-5pm In order to ensure participation of this global cohort, sessions will take place on Thursdays at 3pm Irish Time (8am Honduras, 2pm Sierra Leone, 4pm Zimbabwe, 5pm Ethiopia Uganda). ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); What will I learn? Over these 10 sessions, participants will work with peers globally to develop skills in critical thinking, intercultural communication, project management, teamwork, and the fundamentals of organising and implementing public awareness campaigns. By taking part you will be empowered to: Understand the diverse and interconnected nature of our world. Critically interrogate the systems that underpin opportunities to engage and flourish in society. Reflect upon your own role and the role of others in tackling local and global challenges. Engage in dialogue with others in a spirit of empathy, inclusivity, solidarity and collaboration. Develop the courage, confidence and resilience to speak out for change. Understand the GOAL s work and have the opportunity to connect with GOAL staff. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); What are we looking for in a candidate? To be considered, applicants must meet the following criteria: Current resident of Ireland, Ethiopia, Honduras, Iraq, Sierra Leone, Syria, Turkey, Uganda, or Zimbabwe. Comfortably fluent in English. Between 18 and 25 years old. Available for 10 seminars between May and July. Sessions will fall on Thursdays and Saturdays (see timeline for actual dates). Be open and willing to engage in teamwork in between seminars. Demonstrate basic/emerging understanding of local and global issues. Show clear initiative and desire to be a part of positive change (this could be demonstrated through volunteering, participation in local events, workshop facilitation, tutoring, creative extra-curricular activities, etc.). Interest in participating in artistic methods of learning (this could be demonstrated through a passion for drama, art, creative writing, journalism, music or any other artistic discipline). Reliable access to a device and internet is desired but not essential. If you do not have access to a device, please let us know in your application, and we will endeavour to support your participation. How do I apply? To apply, please use the fields at the bottom of the page to upload the following: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); 1. Your CV or Portfolio Keep it simple, an outline of your educational background and any voluntary and/or professional experience is sufficient. Alternatively, you may include a short portfolio with examples of your work or projects to date. 2. A 500 word cover letter addressing the following: o Tell us about yourself. Why are you applying for this programme, and why do you believe you are a strong fit? ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); o Describe the skills you bring. What strengths or experiences will you contribute, and what do you hope to gain from participating? Applications who do not follow this format will not be accepted. Or Send us a two- minute video explaining why you are applying for this programme and why you believe you would be a strong fit. Describe the skills and strenghts you can contribute, and what you hope to gain from participating. Video submissions are strongly encourage. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Please note that video submissions need to be sent separately to somalley@goal.ie with your full name and country of participation in the email subject line, alongside your CV or portfolio. The CV/portfolio is a mandatory submission alongside the video application. Applications that do not adhere to these requests will not be considered. Apply Now Closing Date Applications close on April 1st. Successful applicants will be notified of selection by May 2nd. If you are not selected to participate in this year s cohort, we encourage you to stay in touch with us for other opportunities and to apply again next year. GOAL s NextGen Youth Diversity and Inclusion GOAL is committed to widening participation and achieving equity of access to non-formal Global Citizenship Education. We welcome applications from participants with diverse identities, socio-economically diverse participants, participants with or without disabilities and participants from all ethnic backgrounds. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Stipend Eligibility In order to support participation, stipends will be available for all successful participants. Stipends are intended to cover costs of internet and data as well as any travel costs incurred during the programme. Stipends of 400 for the duration of the programme are available for all successful participants. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Apply Now Source: https://www.goalglobal.org/nextgen-youth-recruitment-2025/ The post GOAL NextGen Youth Programme 2026: Global Leadership Programme for Young Changemakers (Stipend Available) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNFCCC/UNDP Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0: Global Youth Climate Leadership Programme
summary: Welcome to the Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0, a collaborative effort between UNFCCC s Youth4Capacity programme and UNDP s Youth4Climate initiative (powered by the Italian Ministry of Environment and Energy Security ) ! Young climate leaders worldwide can now apply for the Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0 , an international youth mentorship initiative designed to equip emerging changemakers with the skills, knowledge, and networks needed to drive meaningful climate action. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The programme is a collaborative initiative between the UNFCCC Youth4Capacity Programme and the UNDP Youth4Climate Initiative , powered by the Italian Ministry of Environment and Energy Security . It aims to strengthen youth leadership in climate solutions by providing hands-on training, mentorship, and global networking opportunities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); If you are passionate about climate action, sustainability, youth leadership, and environmental innovation , this programme provides an opportunity to learn directly from experts while collaborating with other youth leaders around the world. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); About the Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme The Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0 brings together a global cohort of youth who are committed to advancing climate solutions in their communities and beyond. Participants will take part in a structured learning journey that includes interactive bootcamps, peer learning opportunities, and collaborative engagement sessions . The programme is designed to help young leaders build practical skills in climate project development, leadership, advocacy, and social innovation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); By the end of the programme, participants will be better equipped to design impactful climate initiatives, mobilize communities, and contribute to global climate action efforts. Programme Structure The programme features three intensive online bootcamps focused on essential skills for climate leadership and project development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); 1. Project Design for Climate Action Participants will learn how to design effective climate projects, including: Developing climate solutions at local and global levels Project planning and implementation strategies Monitoring and evaluating climate initiatives Resource mobilization and fundraising approaches 2. Movement Building and Climate Advocacy This bootcamp focuses on strengthening youth-led climate movements and advocacy efforts through: Leadership and community organizing Communication strategies for climate advocacy Building inclusive and impactful climate movements Collaboration with stakeholders and partners ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); 3. Entrepreneurial Mindsets for Climate Innovation Participants will explore how entrepreneurship can drive climate solutions by learning about: Social entrepreneurship for sustainability Innovative climate business models Problem-solving and systems thinking Scaling climate solutions for greater impact Programme Format The Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme will run for one month and includes: Three online bootcamps 3 4 sessions for each bootcamp Interactive learning with climate experts and practitioners Peer-to-peer engagement through a dedicated online platform Informal networking sessions through Virtual Climate Caf s ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); This immersive learning structure ensures that participants gain both theoretical knowledge and practical tools for advancing climate action. What Participants Will Gain Participants selected for the programme will benefit from a range of learning and networking opportunities, including: Training in climate project design and implementation Skills in leadership, advocacy, and resource mobilization Exposure to global climate experts and practitioners Collaboration with a diverse international youth network Opportunities to exchange ideas through peer-to-peer learning Participation in Virtual Climate Caf networking sessions ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); These experiences are designed to help young leaders strengthen their capacity to develop, lead, and scale climate solutions in their communities. Opportunity to Attend COP31 Outstanding participants in the programme may have the exclusive opportunity to attend COP31 , the United Nations Climate Change Conference. Selected youth may also be invited to present and pitch their climate project or solution during the conference, gaining global visibility and engagement with international climate leaders and stakeholders. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Certificate of Participation Participants who successfully complete the programme will receive an official certificate of participation , recognizing their contribution and commitment to climate leadership and youth-led environmental action. Who Should Apply The programme is ideal for young climate advocates, sustainability leaders, youth organizers, students, and innovators who want to deepen their impact in climate action. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); You should consider applying if you: Are passionate about climate action and environmental sustainability Want to develop skills in project management and leadership Are interested in climate advocacy and movement building Want to collaborate with youth leaders from around the world Have ideas or initiatives focused on climate solutions How to Apply Interested applicants should complete the programme application form HERE . Selected participants will be contacted to join the programme cohort. This is a unique opportunity to learn from climate experts, collaborate with global youth leaders, and strengthen your capacity to lead climate solutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Apply today and become part of a new generation of Climate Catalysts driving global climate action. For more global youth opportunities, fellowships, scholarships, and leadership programmes, visit: www.opportunitiesforyouth.org The post UNFCCC/UNDP Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0: Global Youth Climate Leadership Programme appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: NASA Goddard Summer Internship for High School Students 2026: Applications Open for Students Aged 16+
summary: High school students interested in science, technology, engineering, and space exploration now have an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on experience with one of the world s leading space research institutions. Research Focus: Sustainable Living and Mental Wellbeing One of the defining characteristics of the EM-SLAM doctoral program is its focus on the intersection between mental health and environmental sustainability . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Climate change and ecological disruption are increasingly affecting the psychological wellbeing of individuals and communities. Entry Requirements Applicants must demonstrate strong academic preparation and a clear research interest in the themes of sustainable living and mental wellbeing. title: The Queen’s Commonwealth Writing Competition 2026: Global Writing Contest for Young Writers Across the Commonwealth

About The Queen s Commonwealth Writing Competition The Queen s Commonwealth Writing Competition is one of the flagship youth initiatives of the Royal Commonwealth Society , an organization dedicated to promoting education, equality, sustainability, and global cooperation across Commonwealth nations. Since its inception in the late 19th century, the competition has served as a powerful platform for young voices. It encourages participants to engage with global themes and express their ideas through creative writing. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Over the years, the competition has helped nurture generations of talented writers, many of whom have gone on to achieve remarkable accomplishments in literature, journalism, public service, and academia. The competition has grown significantly in recent years. In 2025 alone, the competition received over 53,000 entries from around the world , demonstrating its global impact and the enthusiasm of young writers eager to share their perspectives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Through this competition, the Royal Commonwealth Society seeks to empower youth by giving them the opportunity to explore important issues affecting their communities and the wider world. 2026 Theme: Common Ground Better Together The theme for the 2026 Queen s Commonwealth Writing Competition is Common Ground: Better Together. The Commonwealth is one of the most diverse international communities in the world, spanning multiple continents and representing a wide array of cultures, languages, landscapes, and generations. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Its member countries range from small island nations to vast and populous states. While these countries differ in geography, history, and cultural traditions, they are connected by shared values and common aspirations for a better future. The theme encourages participants to explore questions such as: What unites communities despite their differences? How can cooperation and partnership drive positive change? What shared challenges do Commonwealth nations face today? How can young people contribute to building stronger and more inclusive communities? ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Participants are encouraged to reflect creatively on how collaboration, mutual understanding, and shared responsibility can help address the challenges facing societies today. By participating in this global contest, young writers join a vibrant international community that celebrates creativity, diversity, and shared human values. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); A Legacy of Distinguished Alumni Over the years, many participants and winners of the Queen s Commonwealth Writing Competition have gone on to achieve remarkable success in literature and beyond. The competition has served as an early platform for talented young writers whose creativity and insight have shaped their future careers. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); These alumni demonstrate how powerful storytelling and thoughtful writing can influence society, inspire change, and open doors to new opportunities. The Royal Commonwealth Society celebrates the achievements of past participants who have used their voices to contribute to meaningful conversations about global issues. The Evolution of the Competition The Queen s Commonwealth Writing Competition continues to evolve to reflect the changing landscape of global storytelling and youth engagement. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Formerly known as the Queen s Commonwealth Essay Competition , the initiative has been reimagined to embrace a broader definition of writing and creativity. All submissions must reflect the 2026 theme Common Ground: Better Together. Global Reach of the Commonwealth Community The Commonwealth represents one of the most diverse global communities, connecting countries across Africa, Asia, Europe, the Americas, and the Pacific. Despite differences in culture, geography, and history, Commonwealth nations share common values rooted in cooperation, mutual respect, and a commitment to improving lives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); The Queen s Commonwealth Writing Competition celebrates this diversity while encouraging young people to reflect on the shared values that unite communities. title: AmaliTech AWS re/Start Programme 2026: Fully Funded Cloud Computing Training for Ghanaian STEM Graduates (Free Certification, Stipends, and Job Pathways)

title: Schwarzman Academy Fellowship 2026–2027 at Chatham House: Fully Funded 10-Month Global Policy Leadership Fellowship in London
summary: Applications are now open for the prestigious Schwarzman Academy Fellowship 2026 2027 hosted by Chatham House, the Royal Institute of International Affairs in London. This highly competitive fellowship offers emerging global leaders the opportunity to conduct policy-relevant research, engage with global experts, and develop leadership skills through an intensive ten-month fellowship programme. The fellowship is offered through The Queen Elizabeth II Academy for Leadership and the Next Generation at Chatham House and is designed to support early- to mid-career professionals who are committed to shaping evidence-based solutions to global challenges. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The programme will begin in October 2026 and will run for ten months. During this period, fellows will work closely with Chatham House researchers, participate in policy dialogues and events, and contribute to research outputs that address pressing international issues. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); The application deadline for the Schwarzman Academy Fellowship 2026 2027 is 20 March 2026 at 5 pm GMT. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); About Chatham House Chatham House, officially known as the Royal Institute of International Affairs, is one of the world s leading independent policy institutes. Based in London, the institution has built a global reputation for conducting high-quality research, facilitating dialogue on international affairs, and promoting evidence-based policy solutions. The organization s mission is to help build a sustainably secure, prosperous, and just world. Through research programmes, policy dialogues, and international partnerships, Chatham House addresses major global challenges including geopolitical conflict, economic transformation, environmental sustainability, global health, and international governance. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The Queen Elizabeth II Academy for Leadership and the Next Generation was established to cultivate the next generation of global policy leaders. The Academy provides structured fellowship programmes designed to equip participants with leadership capabilities, analytical skills, and networks needed to address complex international challenges. Overview of the Schwarzman Academy Fellowship The Schwarzman Academy Fellowship is a ten-month in-person fellowship that provides an exceptional platform for Schwarzman Scholars completing their master s degree to further develop their expertise in global policy research and leadership. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Fellows will join a cohort of international professionals drawn from academia, civil society, government, business, and media sectors. Participants will contribute to Chatham House research programmes while also developing their leadership capabilities through a structured Leadership Masterclass Programme. The fellowship combines independent research with collaborative engagement across Chatham House s global network of experts, policymakers, and practitioners. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Fellowship Structure and Responsibilities During the ten-month fellowship, Schwarzman Academy Fellows will engage in several core activities that support both their professional development and the research mission of Chatham House. Fellows will conduct independent research on global policy themes or geographic regions aligned with the research priorities of Chatham House programmes. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Participants will contribute to Chatham House research initiatives, publications, and events, offering their expertise and insights into key global policy debates. Fellows will also collaborate with a broad network of scholars, policymakers, journalists, and civil society leaders connected to Chatham House. In addition, participants will take part in the Leadership Masterclass Programme, which is designed to strengthen policy leadership skills and prepare fellows for influential roles in global affairs. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Leadership Masterclass Programme The Leadership Masterclass Programme is an integral part of the Schwarzman Academy Fellowship. It provides fellows with structured opportunities to develop leadership capabilities and strategic thinking skills relevant to international policy environments. Through seminars, workshops, and mentoring sessions, fellows will build skills in leadership, policy communication, media engagement, and strategic analysis. Participants will also receive writing support, career mentoring, and coaching to help them translate research insights into impactful policy contributions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); The programme aims to cultivate a new generation of global leaders capable of shaping policy solutions in increasingly complex geopolitical environments. Research Focus Areas Applicants must propose research that aligns with the priorities of one of the Chatham House research programmes. These programmes focus on major global policy issues and regional developments. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Research themes include topics related to Africa s digital transformation, governance and digital public infrastructure, the geopolitics of digital connectivity, and digital trade negotiations. Other areas include the global energy transition and strategies for achieving sustainable resource use. Applicants may also focus on European strategic sovereignty, industrial policy integration, and Europe s engagement with rising global powers. In the area of global economic policy, research may explore the future of the US dollar in the international monetary system or the impact of artificial intelligence on the global economy. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Global health research topics include African health sovereignty, geopolitical dynamics in health diplomacy, and strengthening global health security systems. Additional areas include international law, security studies, conflict resolution, Latin American geopolitics, Middle East governance and regional dynamics, Russian political economy, Central Asian transformation, UK foreign policy strategy, and US global economic and security policies. Applicants are encouraged to develop innovative research proposals that align with Chatham House s mission and contribute to evidence-based policy solutions. Benefits of the Fellowship Schwarzman Academy Fellows receive a comprehensive benefits package designed to support their participation in the fellowship and their professional development. Fellows receive a monthly stipend of 2,565 to cover living expenses in London, including accommodation, utilities, food, and transport. Chatham House also covers relocation costs including travel, visa fees, and accommodation support. Successful applicants requiring a visa will be sponsored under the UK Temporary Work Government Authorised Exchange visa scheme. The fellowship also covers the UK Immigration Health Surcharge, though dental, optical care, and prescription costs are not included. Fellows are provided with research funding to support fieldwork, outreach activities, policy events, and publications. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Participants also gain full membership to Chatham House during the fellowship, providing access to exclusive events, research resources, and expert networks. After completing the programme, fellows receive a five-year Chatham House membership. Professional Development and Networking The fellowship provides extensive networking opportunities with global policymakers, academics, and practitioners. Participants engage with Chatham House staff, Academy faculty, and a global alumni network that includes leaders across government, international organizations, and policy institutions. These networks often open pathways to careers in international organizations, government advisory roles, think tanks, and global policy institutions. Location and Programme Format The Schwarzman Academy Fellowship is based in London and operates on a hybrid working model. Fellows are expected to spend a minimum of two days per week in the Chatham House office, though some weeks may require additional in-person participation for leadership programme activities and events. The fellowship is a full-time commitment and participants cannot undertake other work or academic study during the programme. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(139); }); Application Requirements Applicants must meet specific academic and professional criteria to be eligible for the Schwarzman Academy Fellowship. Applicants must be current Schwarzman Scholars who are due to complete their Schwarzman Scholars master s degree in 2026. Candidates must have experience conducting independent research and must hold at least a bachelor s degree or have equivalent professional experience. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(140); }); Applicants should demonstrate leadership potential, a strong commitment to international policy work, and alignment with the mission and values of Chatham House. Proficiency in English is required. Applicants must also submit a research proposal that aligns with one of the Chatham House research programmes and demonstrates originality and policy relevance. If you require assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, please contact us at academy@chathamhouse.org. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(141); }); Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the Schwarzman Academy Fellowship 2026 2027 is 20 March 2026 at 5 pm GMT. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, and early submission is strongly encouraged. Applicants will be notified of the outcome within four weeks after the application deadline. How to Apply Interested applicants must complete the official application form and submit their CV along with a research proposal and supporting application materials. Learn more and apply HERE . For questions regarding the fellowship, applicants may contact the Academy team via email at academy@chathamhouse.org . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(142); }); Conclusion The Schwarzman Academy Fellowship at Chatham House represents an exceptional opportunity for emerging global policy leaders to deepen their expertise, conduct impactful research, and strengthen their leadership capabilities. Through research collaboration, leadership development, and engagement with one of the world s most respected international affairs institutions, fellows gain the skills and networks necessary to influence global policy debates and contribute to solutions for the world s most pressing challenges. Click HERE to discover more Fellowship opportunities on OFY. title: Apply Now: Marine Conservation Finance Research Call – WIOMSA MARG 2026
summary: Are you an early-career researcher or graduate student working on marine science, conservation, or ocean finance? The Western Indian Ocean Marine Science Association (WIOMSA), in partnership with the Blue Nature Alliance (BNA), has issued a thematic research call under its Marine Research Grant (MARG) Programme to strengthen how marine conservation finance is understood and practiced across the Western Indian Ocean (WIO) region . This call focused on marine conservation finance invites applied, field-based research proposals that explore ways to make long-term financing systems more sustainable, resilient, and equitable. With marine conservation ambitions expanding across the region, including commitments to Global Biodiversity Framework targets and 30 30 objectives, there is a pressing need for robust evidence and innovative financial strategies to support protected areas, coastal communities, and blue economy initiatives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); What This Call Is About Traditional funding models for marine conservation often rely on short-term or project-based contributions that leave critical programs exposed to economic and geopolitical risks. Fragmented revenue, fluctuating budgets, and narrow funding streams can undermine long-term planning for Marine Protected Areas (MPAs), Locally Managed Marine Areas (LMMAs), and other essential conservation frameworks. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); To help address these challenges, WIOMSA and the Blue Nature Alliance are seeking research that builds a strong evidence base on financing systems for marine conservation in the Western Indian Ocean. The aim is to generate practical insights that can inform national, regional, and policy frameworks for durable and diversified conservation finance . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Applicants are encouraged to explore topics including, but not limited to: Quantifying financing needs and identifying gaps in current funding frameworks Assessing potential revenue diversification strategies and financial resilience Evaluating governance and institutional structures that support effective finance systems Linking financial stability to measurable conservation outcomes and performance metrics Analysing the implications of economic shocks on marine conservation financing Why This Research Matters Marine conservation in the Western Indian Ocean a region that includes countries such as Kenya, Tanzania, Mozambique, Seychelles, and Madagascar is integral to both ecological resilience and the wellbeing of coastal communities. Healthy marine ecosystems underpin fisheries, tourism, coastal protection, and cultural heritage. However, even well-designed conservation programs can falter without reliable, coordinated financial systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Thematic research supported under this call will help bridge the gap between conservation planning and financial sustainability. By producing evidence on financing gaps, governance structures, and revenue diversification strategies, researchers contribute to a more predictable, equitable, and impact-oriented conservation finance landscape in the WIO. This work can influence policy decisions, investment priorities, and long-term sustainability strategies for marine ecosystems across the region. For early career and postgraduate researchers, this call offers not only funding but also the chance to be part of a growing community focused on ocean sustainability and finance innovation a field increasingly recognised as critical to achieving global biodiversity and climate goals. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Eligibility and Who Should Apply This call is specifically targeted at: Early Career Ocean Professionals (ECOPs) Ongoing Master s and PhD students currently enrolled at recognised institutions within the WIO region Individuals whose research aligns with strengthening marine conservation finance dynamics in the region Proposals should be field-based and designed to generate data and insights that inform actionable strategies for improving financing systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Grant Support and Structure Successful proposals will receive support for field-based research activities, with grants covering expenses directly related to: Data collection and fieldwork Research materials and logistics Stakeholder engagement efforts tied to the project ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Selected research proposals will receive financial support to carry out field-based research related to marine conservation finance, including costs for fieldwork, stakeholder engagement, and data collection Timeline How to Apply Deadline for proposals: 30 March 2026 Applicants should complete the online submission form provided by WIOMSA. Learn more and apply on the official page . Interested researchers are encouraged to read the full call for proposals carefully to understand priority themes, eligibility criteria, and submission requirements. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Looking for more opportunities? Explore the latest global calls on the Opportunities for Youth website. The post Apply Now: Marine Conservation Finance Research Call – WIOMSA MARG 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for DASIE 2026 – Fully Funded Data Analytics Immersion at Carnegie Mellon University
summary: Are you an undergraduate student curious about how data shapes scientific discovery and research careers? The Data Analytics for Science Immersion Experience (DASIE) at Carnegie Mellon University (CMU) offers a fully funded, immersive summer experience designed to introduce students to the real-world use of data analytics across scientific disciplines. The program brings together students from accredited U.S. colleges and universities including those on student visas to explore the intersection of data and science while building community, learning from experts, and contemplating future academic and professional paths. This nine-day residential experience takes place at CMU s Pittsburgh campus in May 2026, and is tailored for current undergraduate students and first-year master s students interested in data-informed scientific work. All travel, lodging, transportation, and meals are covered, and participants receive a stipend, making DASIE not just an educational opportunity but an accessible one. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); What DASIE Is All About DASIE is a weeklong immersion experience hosted by the Mellon College of Science (MCS) at Carnegie Mellon University. It is designed to introduce students to the role of data analytics in science by connecting them with faculty, researchers, and practitioners who use data in their work. The program recognizes the growing importance of data skills across scientific fields and aims to help students understand how data informs questions, decisions, and innovation in scientific research. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); During DASIE, students take part in structured tutorial sessions, workshops, presentations, and discussions led by CMU faculty and experts from organizations such as the Pittsburgh Supercomputing Center . These sessions showcase examples of data-driven scientific work and highlight career and academic paths that data analytics can unlock. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); In addition to scheduled program activities, DASIE provides space for participants to network with peers from a range of institutions, meet faculty and staff, and build community. Group activities encourage reflection on students own interests and goals while exposing them to diverse uses of data across scientific contexts. Why DASIE Matters As data becomes central to nearly every scientific discipline from genomics to climate modeling, from drug discovery to astrophysics students with data literacy and analytical skills gain a competitive edge in both academic and professional arenas. Programs like DASIE help bridge the gap between classroom learning and real-world scientific data practices at world-class research institutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Beyond the technical exposure, DASIE offers students the chance to meet faculty, industry professionals, and fellow students with shared interests. These interactions help participants expand their understanding of how data shapes decision-making and scientific inquiry, and can inspire the pursuit of graduate work or careers in data-rich environments. Students who have participated in past iterations of DASIE report gaining clarity about their academic goals, building confidence in their capabilities, and connecting with communities and pathways they might not have otherwise explored. Some have even gone on to launch student organizations or pursue advanced studies in data science and analytics. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); What Participants Experience The DASIE program runs from May 12 to May 20, 2026 , with on-campus programming from May 13 19. Session days typically begin early and include a mix of instructional content, hands-on activities, and professional development. A group activity off campus fosters community and adds a fun learning dimension outside the classroom. Who Should Apply DASIE is open to current undergraduate students and first-year master s students from accredited U.S. institutions. title: Study at SOAS University of London in 2026: Apply for Master’s and PhD Programmes (Global Postgraduate Opportunities)
summary: SOAS University of London has officially opened applications for its 2026 postgraduate programmes, including both Master s degrees and Doctoral (MPhil/PhD) research programmes. English Language Requirements Applicants must demonstrate English proficiency sufficient to engage fully with their academic programme. Accepted evidence may include: IELTS TOEFL Other recognized English language qualifications If an applicant s English language level does not meet the requirements, preparatory courses are available through the International Foundation Courses and English Language Studies (IFCELS) department. These preparatory programmes help international students strengthen their academic English skills and meet admission requirements. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Step 3: Submit Your Application Applicants must submit the required documents through the online application system. Required documentation includes: Academic Documents Degree transcripts showing all modules and marks Degree certificate (if the programme has already been completed) Interim transcript (for students currently studying) All documents must be submitted in English or accompanied by certified translations. Additional Required Documents Passport identity page Curriculum Vitae (CV) or resume Supporting statement (approximately 1,000 words) The personal statement should explain: Motivation for studying the programme Relevant experience Career goals Suitability for the programme ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Applicants are advised to write their statements in their own words and avoid using templates. References are not required for most Master s programmes but may be included if desired. Step 4: After Submitting Your Application SOAS aims to process complete applications within approximately four weeks. During the evaluation process: Admissions staff review the application materials Additional information may be requested if necessary ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Successful applicants receive an offer and are directed to the next steps required for enrolment. Explore Funding Options Students should investigate available funding opportunities, including scholarships, studentships, and grants. title: Apply Now: Pulitzer Center Journalism Grants on Health in the Global South

title: Australian Government Research Training Program – Fully Funded Research Degree Support
summary: For students passionate about advancing knowledge through research, Australia offers one of the world s most significant government-backed scholarship frameworks: the Research Training Program (RTP) . Administered through the Australian Government s Research Block Grants, the RTP provides funding to support domestic and international students undertaking higher degrees by research (HDRs) such as PhDs or research-focused Master s degrees at eligible Australian universities. Whether you envision a career in academia, industry research, policy analysis, or development innovation, the RTP can unlock financial support that makes pursuing a research degree in Australia both feasible and attractive. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); What Is the Research Training Program? The Research Training Program (RTP) is part of Australia s broader Research Block Grants framework , which also includes the Research Support Program. While the Research Support Program helps Australian universities cover systemic research costs, the RTP specifically targets the training and support of research students. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Under the RTP, eligible higher education providers receive government funding they can use to offer scholarships to students undertaking higher degrees by research including research doctorates and research Masters degrees. These scholarships provide a combination of financial support, including: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Tuition fee offsets that cover the cost of university research tuition Stipends to assist with general living costs Allowances for ancillary research-related expenses (such as relocation, printing, or health insurance) Different universities handle the application and selection process for RTP scholarships themselves. Prospective candidates must therefore contact their chosen institution directly to learn how to apply. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Why This Opportunity Matters Research degrees are gateways to deep expertise and innovation that shape industries, public policy, and scientific discovery. However, pursuing a higher degree by research (HDR) such as a PhD or research master s can be financially and logistically demanding. Tuition costs, living expenses, health insurance, and the long time commitment required often pose significant barriers to talented researchers. The Australian Government s Research Training Program makes advanced research study more accessible by alleviating these financial pressures. RTP scholarships help students focus on their research contributions rather than their costs of living, enabling deeper engagement with their work. Scholarships can cover tuition fees entirely, provide tax-free stipends for living costs, and include useful allowances for ancillary research support making study in Australia a viable option for both domestic and international candidates. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Moreover, the RTP supports students in training environments that foster collaboration with industry and research end-users, helping bridge academic research with real-world impact. Through partnerships between universities and industry, RTP recipients can gain valuable experience, build professional networks, and enhance their long-term career prospects beyond academia whether in government, enterprise, or the non-profit sector. In a global landscape where research excellence is increasingly tied to innovation and socioeconomic progress, the RTP stands as a strategic investment in future leaders and problem-solvers. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); How Long Are RTP Scholarships? RTP scholarships support students for the duration of their research degrees, subject to guidelines and university discretion: Research doctorate candidates : Typically supported for 3 to 4 years full-time (or 6 to 8 years part-time) Research Master s candidates : Supported for up to 2 years full-time (or up to 4 years part-time) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); The exact period of support is determined by the university offering the RTP scholarship and the rules set out in Australia s Higher Education Support (Commonwealth Scholarships) Guidelines, which were updated in 2025. Who Can Apply? To be eligible for an RTP scholarship, students must: Be enrolled (or intending to enrol) in an accredited research doctorate or research Master s degree (HDR) at an eligible Australian university Meet individual university entry requirements, which typically include academic transcripts, research proposals, and supervisor support Both domestic and international students can be offered RTP scholarships, though the application, selection criteria, and availability vary by institution. Students may apply for RTP support at multiple universities, and universities may consider prior study, research experience, and publications when selecting recipients. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Because RTP scholarships are administered at the university level, interested applicants should contact the postgraduate research office of their chosen institution to confirm eligibility, specific application dates, and required materials. How to Apply RTP scholarships are awarded by eligible Australian higher education providers. Prospective HDR students should: Choose an Australian university offering a research doctorate or research master s aligned with their interests. Contact the postgraduate research office for details on the university s RTP application procedure. Submit required documents (e.g., research proposal, academic records, supervisor support) by the university s deadline. Learn more about RTP scholarships on the official website. Each university s process may differ, so early inquiry and preparation are essential. Looking for more opportunities? Explore the latest global calls on the Opportunities for Youth website . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); The post Australian Government Research Training Program – Fully Funded Research Degree Support appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: SYPALA 2026: IMANI Opens Applications for Africa’s Premier Student & Young Professionals Policy Seminar
summary: The IMANI Center for Policy Education has officially opened applications for the 12th Students and Young Professionals African Liberty Academy (SYPALA) 2026 , its flagship pan-African summer seminar designed to cultivate the next generation of African policy and business leaders. SYPALA is a highly competitive residential program that brings together outstanding undergraduate students and recent graduates from across Africa who are passionate about strengthening economic resilience and advancing inclusive governance within their countries. Admission into the program is based solely on the quality of the applicant s essay , making intellectual rigor, originality, and policy insight the core selection criteria. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About SYPALA SYPALA is a four-day immersive academic seminar organized by the IMANI Center for Policy Education . title: African Women’s Development Fund Call for African Feminist Consultants 2026
summary: The African Women s Development Fund (AWDF) has announced a call for African feminist consultants to support its movement accompaniment and strengthening initiatives across the continent. This paid consultancy opportunity invites experienced feminist practitioners with expertise in collective care, digital security, crisis response, advocacy, and resilience building to apply before 20 March 2026 . In line with its commitment to fostering flourishing feminist movements, AWDF has identified key strategic pathways for accompaniment and is inviting experienced practitioners to apply for one or more specialized assignments. Each consultancy requires distinct thematic expertise, facilitation approaches, and varying levels of engagement. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Available Consultancy Assignments 1. Collective Care as Political Strategy A two-day workshop designed to reposition care from an individual responsibility to a radical, systemic organizational practice. The training frames wellbeing as a frontline defense against backlash and repression. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); 2. Digital Security and Collective Safety A four-day in-person training focused on strengthening practical digital security and data protection skills. Research and Advocacy Teach-In A five-day in-person strategic retreat where feminist research meets political action. Priority expertise: Feminists operating in French and Portuguese-speaking contexts . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); 5. Peer Learning Exchange Programme A six-month online journey featuring monthly facilitated peer learning circles covering: Feminist governance Resource mobilization Digital security Collective care Advocacy 6. Resilience Building Accompaniment A six-month online cyclical learning programme addressing: Advocacy in hostile environments Collective care practices Development of Organizational Resilience Action Plans 7. Integrated Resource Directory and Toolkit Development of a living, accessible ecosystem of tools and contextual knowledge aimed at strengthening operational resilience and strategic response capacity for French and Portuguese-speaking women s rights movements. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Who Should Apply? AWDF is seeking African feminist consultants with: Proven thematic expertise relevant to the selected assignment(s) Strong facilitation and movement-building experience Contextual understanding of feminist organizing across Africa Capacity to deliver both in-person and virtual programming Applicants may apply for one or multiple assignments depending on their expertise. Application Process Interested practitioners must submit: Technical Expression of Interest Detailed methodology Proposed pathway(s) for movement accompaniment Financial Proposal Send you applications to Consultants@awdf.org Deadline: 20 March 2026 For detailed scope and requirements, applicants are encouraged to review the official Terms of Reference . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Why This Opportunity Matters This call presents a significant opportunity for African feminist practitioners to contribute to strengthening women s rights movements through structured accompaniment, collective learning, and resilience-building strategies. By centering care, digital safety, crisis response, and advocacy, AWDF continues to invest in sustainable feminist futures across the continent.
